prompt_text:
Analyze the following java classes for violations of the Dependency Inversion Principle (DIP).

For each class you are given:
- Its full source body
- Its base class bodies (where available in the repo)
- The file's imports
- Short headers of sibling classes in the same file

Use the inheritance and import context to reason about dependencies and substitutability.

## Principle Definition
High-level modules should not depend on low-level modules; both should depend on abstractions. Abstractions ...
raw_response:
{
  "findings": [
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/ackedqueue/Queue.java",
      "entity_name": "Queue",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 55,
      "line_end": 980,
      "principle": "DIP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "Queue directly instantiates concrete implementation FileCheckpointIO",
      "reasoning": "The Queue class directly instantiates FileCheckpointIO in its constructor. This creates a hard dependency on a specific implementation of checkpoint I/O, preventing the use of alternative storage strategies or mocking for unit testing."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/ackedqueue/Queue.java",
      "entity_name": "Queue.openPages",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 169,
      "line_end": 265,
      "principle": "DIP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "Queue directly instantiates concrete implementation MmapPageIOV2",
      "reasoning": "The openPages method directly instantiates MmapPageIOV2. This couples the high-level Queue logic to a specific file-based I/O implementation, violating the principle that high-level modules should depend on abstractions."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/execution/AbstractPipelineExt.java",
      "entity_name": "AbstractPipelineExt.createDeadLetterQueueWriterFromSettings",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 323,
      "line_end": 338,
      "principle": "DIP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "AbstractPipelineExt directly depends on the static factory DeadLetterQueueFactory",
      "reasoning": "The pipeline logic relies on a static factory to create DLQ writers. This prevents the injection of different writer implementations and tightly couples the pipeline to the specific DeadLetterQueueFactory implementation."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/common/io/DeadLetterQueueWriter.java",
      "entity_name": "DeadLetterQueueWriter",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 109,
      "line_end": 814,
      "principle": "DIP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "DeadLetterQueueWriter directly instantiates RecordIOWriter",
      "reasoning": "The writer directly instantiates RecordIOWriter in nextWriter(). This hard-codes the dependency on a specific I/O writer implementation, making it difficult to swap or mock the underlying record writing mechanism."
    }
  ]
}

findings.description: Queue directly instantiates concrete implementation FileCheckpointIO
findings.reasoning: The Queue class directly instantiates FileCheckpointIO in its constructor. This creates a hard dependency on a specific implementation of checkpoint I/O, preventing the use of alternative storage strategies or mocking for unit testing.

findings.description: Queue directly instantiates concrete implementation MmapPageIOV2
findings.reasoning: The openPages method directly instantiates MmapPageIOV2. This couples the high-level Queue logic to a specific file-based I/O implementation, violating the principle that high-level modules should depend on abstractions.

findings.description: AbstractPipelineExt directly depends on the static factory DeadLetterQueueFactory
findings.reasoning: The pipeline logic relies on a static factory to create DLQ writers. This prevents the injection of different writer implementations and tightly couples the pipeline to the specific DeadLetterQueueFactory implementation.

The writer directly instantiates RecordIOWriter in nextWriter(). This hard-codes the dependency on a specific I/O writer implementation, making it difficult to swap or mock the underlying record writing mechanism.
